mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, tight labor markets force wage growth that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals synchronized demand-pull inflation and robust economic momentum, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, any month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 0.3% triggers a repricing of inflation expectations that compresses equity valuations, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline following the specified inflation spike when rates are near zero, or the forward return differential between triggered and non-triggered periods is less than 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding corporate revenue bases, which mechanically supports higher S&P 500 earnings and generates positive forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive association with quarterly GDP growth revisions, or the rolling Spearman correlation drops below 0.0 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) declines by more than 1.0% over three consecutive months, it signals a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline when INDPRO exhibits the specified three-month decline pattern.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth (PAYEMS) exceeding 250,000 per month alongside a surge in residential housing starts (HOUST) above 600,000 units indicates broad-based economic acceleration that fuels risk-on equity positioning over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of historical instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid consumer credit contraction (RSAFS monthly decline > 2%), a simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate accelerates discount rate hikes, severely compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM change < -2% AND FEDFUNDS MoM change > 0.05%
failure_condition: Equity valuations do not contract following the specified credit and rate shock combination, or forward returns are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean.
